title: Quantum Algebra Growth Universality Classification
description: Comparison of classical vs quantum Lie algebra dimension for N=3, d=1, level 3 across multiple potentials

results.notes: Finite algebra (dim 15), closes at level 2. No quantum corrections (quadratic Hamiltonians).
Quantum corrections exist at L3 but are linearly dependent on classical generators.
The original discovery. 117th generator is negative semi-definite with P_3 angular structure.
Calogero-Moser potential. Known integrable in 1D. Quantum algebra still grows by 1.
Showed Jahn-Teller ring in gap landscape. Quantum growth confirmed.
Newly supported potential. Quantum growth confirmed.

conclusion: The quantum algebra grows by exactly 1 dimension for ALL singular potentials (1/r^n, n>=1) and does NOT grow for polynomial potentials (r^n). The singular structure at r=0 (encoded via the u_ij = 1/r_ij substitution) is what enables the quantum correction to be algebraically independent.
log_r_note: log(r) could not be tested with the polynomial matrix approach because log(u_ij) is transcendental. Requires a separate methodology.
implication: The 117th generator phenomenon is universal across all inverse-power-law singular potentials, not specific to 1/r. This suggests a structural theorem: the Moyal bracket on the u_ij coordinate algebra always produces exactly one new direction at level 3.
